channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Musk writes op-ed backing far-right German political party
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk penned an op-ed further expressing support for the far-right German political party Alternative for Germany (AfD). In response, the paper’s commentary editor resigned in protest.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Drought, fires and deforestation battered Amazon rainforest in 2024
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — 2024 was a brutal year for the Amazon rainforest, with rampant wildfires and extreme drought ravaging large parts of a biome that’s a critical counterweight to climate change. A warming climate fed drought that in turn fed the worst year for fires since 2005.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IDF finds Hezbollah weapons cache in underground tunnel: video
item.description: The Israeli military said it discovered and destroyed an underground tunnel system used by Hezbollah to hide weapons used to attack Israel in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kazakhstan plane crash survivors say they heard bangs before aircraft went down, Putin issues statement
item.description: Survivors of the Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed in Kazakhstan on Christmas Day say they heard at least one loud bang before the aircraft crashed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel detains the director of one of northern Gaza's last functioning hospitals during a raid
item.description: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel’s army has detained the director of one of northern Gaza's last functioning hospitals.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Indian Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, who instituted economic reforms, cremated in New Delhi
item.description: Manmohan Singh, the former Indian prime minister widely regarded as the architect of the country’s economic reform program, has been cremated after a state funeral.
item.dc:creator: Aijaz Hussain, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Leon Panetta suggests it's 'pretty clear' Russia 'responsible' for Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash
item.description: Former Secretary of Defense and CIA director Leon Panetta speculated that Russian air defenses may have caused the crash of an Azerbaijan Airlines plane in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Israeli airstrikes hit a Yemen airport as a jet with hundreds onboard was landing, UN official says
item.description: The top U.N. humanitarian official in Yemen says Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen’s main airport as a civilian Airbus 320 with hundreds of passengers on board was landing this week.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Azerbaijani and U.S. officials suggest plane that crashed may have been hit by weapons fire
item.description: Russian air defense systems may have brought down an Azerbaijani airliner this week, a U.S. official said Friday after an Azerbaijani minister also suggested the plane was hit by a weapon, citing expert analysis and survivor accounts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A 9th telecoms firm has been hit by a massive Chinese espionage campaign, the White House says
item.description: A top White House official says a ninth U.S. telecoms firm has been confirmed to have been hacked as part of a sprawling Chinese espionage campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli soldiers burn a hospital in Gaza
item.description: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israeli soldiers stormed and burned a hospital after forcibly removing staff and patients.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Armed for survival: How Oct 7 Hamas massacre transformed gun culture in Israel
item.description: In the wake of the unprecedented October. 7 Hamas terrorist attacks, Israelis are having to grapple with the new reality of firearm ownership in a country where owning guns was once uncommon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Houthi rebels in Yemen's capital while the WHO chief says he was meters away
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A new round of Israeli airstrikes in Yemen on Thursday targeted the Houthi rebel-held capital and multiple ports, while the World Health Organization's director-general said the bombardment occurred nearby as he prepared to board a flight in Sanaa, with a crew member injured.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aviation experts say Russia's air defense fire likely caused Azerbaijan plane crash as nation mourns
item.description: Aviation experts said Thursday that Russian air defense fire was likely responsible for the Azerbaijani plane crash the day before that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China probes personal disputes after mass killings. Many fear further infringement on freedoms
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The order came from the top. China's leader Xi Jinping wants the recent spree of mass killings that shocked the country not to happen again. He ordered local governments to prevent future “extreme cases.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli attorney general orders probe into report that alleged Netanyahu's wife harassed opponents
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s attorney general has ordered police to open an investigation into Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s wife on suspicion of harassing political opponents and a witness in the Israeli leader’s corruption trial.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden arrives in St. Croix for last vacation as president
item.description: President Biden on Thursday arrived in St. Croix for his last vacation as president before leaving office next month. The president left the White House the day after Christmas with first lady Jill Biden. He arrived in St. Croix in the U.S. Virgin Islands around 2 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: NATO calls for investigation into Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: NATO called for a formal investigation Thursday into the Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash that killed 38 people in Kazakhstan. “Our thoughts and prayers are with the families and victims of #AzerbaijanAirlines flight #J28243.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Russia suspected in Azerbaijan Airlines crash; black boxes recovered
item.description: The U.S. officials added that if the preliminary information proves to be true, it would further underscore Russian recklessness in its invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Moscow on Thursday warned against speculation that Russian air defenses may have caused the crash of an Azerbaijan Airlines plane in Kazakhstan this week that killed 38 people.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Russia downplays speculation over deadly Azerbaijan Airlines crash as report lays blame for downed plane
item.description: Officials in Russia and Kazakhstan are urging people to wait for an investigation into the deadly Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash before assigning blame.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US citizen imprisoned in Russia given new 15-year sentence in wake of espionage conviction
item.description: A Russian-born U.S. citizen who was already behind bars in Russia on a bribery conviction has been handed a new 15-year sentence for espionage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: White House briefs Trump administration on nuclear threats
item.description: On Christmas Eve, tensions in the Holy Land persist as fears over nuclear weapon production in Iran and Pakistan amid the ongoing Israel war.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Police officer dressed as the 'Grinch' steals Christmas spirit during drug bust
item.description: The Peruvian National Police canceled Christmas early for three alleged drug traffickers during a holiday-themed takedown that was captured on camera.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran recruiting children to attack Israeli targets across Europe amid setbacks: report
item.description: Iran has been hiring young people in Europe to attack Israeli and Jewish targets in countries across the continent, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 2 Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
